FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional bank M&A activity picks up with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while regulatory headwinds mount on immigration lending and Fed rate expectations shift higher.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaiian bank's return to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
New Fed rate-hike odds rising for July on oil-price tensions; direct headwind for bank NIM compression and deposit costs.
Regulators issue guidance requiring banks to monitor and control lending risks tied to unauthorized workers—signals tighter credit standards ahead for immigrant-heavy loan portfolios.
JPMorgan's Lake retirement after board named co-presidents Petno and Rohrbaugh; Dimon calls it 'natural change' but signals potential CEO succession track clarifying.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments' $580B CalPERS acquisition headlines a busy day in wealth management, with RIA consolidation accelerating and operational restructuring underway across the sector.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquires Russell Investments for multi-asset capability while Russell sheds debt and converts to non-profit governance—a Vanguard-style playbook that could reshape its competitive positioning.

RIA consolidation hits overdrive: Hightower posts record organic growth, Choreo closes its fourth deal post-SpaceX win, and ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform as market fragmentation persists.
Aspen-backed Summitry ($4B RIA) undergoes leadership refresh with founder Colin Higgins moving to executive chairman and two executives elevated to co-CEOs after 22 years.
Carson Group restructures go-to-market into separate W-2 and independent RIA channels to sharpen focus and boost organic growth amid consolidation wave.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Structural shifts in insurance markets: UK opens captive regime, reinsurers race to diversify capital access, while power and ag risks reshape underwriting.

bullets.text: UK regulators propose new captive insurance rules to unlock multi-billion-pound alternative insurance market, signaling openness to non-traditional structures.
AI data-center demand has pushed PJM power costs up 60% and capacity auction missed supply targets across 13-state grid serving 65M people—major exposure for energy-heavy underwriting.
Cedents now demanding reinsurers with dual access to traditional and alternative capital; Guy Carpenter's renewal report shows structural shift in buyer behavior toward capacity diversification.
Canada suspends Texas livestock exports over New World screwworm detection—ag insurance impact, though most forecasters still predict below-normal 2026 Atlantic hurricane season.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Stripe/Advent eyeing PayPal at $53B+ while CMBS distress signals servicer action ahead; digital payments infrastructure reshaping across CBDCs and blockchain rails.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent jointly bid for PayPal at >$53B valuation—would be fintech mega-consolidation reshaping payments competitive landscape.
CMBS special servicing rate climbed in June 2026 as retail, office, and lodging loans deteriorate; expect servicer workouts ahead.
ECB green-lit 36 PSPs for digital euro pilot and Emirates NBD went live on Partior blockchain network—central bank and tokenized settlement infrastructure both accelerating.

PE & Deals.lede: GP financing innovation and secondaries expansion dominate—Arctos deploys novel self-liquidating structure while Moelis and Carlyle tap into growing LP appetites.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closed a $6.2B GP financing deal using a 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure, a debut deployment that allows LPs to receive capital returns while maintaining portfolio exposure.
Moelis hired an Evercore VP to build out LP-led secondaries with a full four-person team transfer, signaling aggressive expansion into this high-growth segment.
Carlyle AlpInvest oversubscribed its second single-asset secondary fund at $1.7B (target: $1B), confirming LP appetite for curated, transparent secondary strategies.

Markets & Macro.lede: Stripe and Advent's $53bn PayPal bid dominates; bank earnings season kicks off amid regulatory tailwinds but cycle-peak concerns.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent Capital jointly bid $53bn for PayPal with shares up 19% pre-market—a major PE-backed fintech consolidation play that would reshape payments.
Bank earnings season starts Tuesday with major lenders reporting amid high expectations; EU moves to relax capital requirements signal global deregulation momentum benefiting the sector.
Major U.S. banks posted record Q2 earnings on IB/trading strength, but geopolitical risk, rate-cut bets, and CRE exposure pose downside risks to the earnings cycle peak.
Altice International faces €2bn debt default accusations as Patrick Drahi's asset transfers allegedly violated covenant terms—a cautionary tale on leverage timing.

Newsletters.lede: Wall Street posts record revenues amid trading volatility and M&A surge, while the Fed signals inflation hawkishness and ETF innovation accelerates across bond and structured products.

bullets.text: Wall Street banks logged record revenues driven by Iran-related trading volatility and elevated M&A activity, with investment banking units seeing outsized gains.
Fed Governor Kevin Warsh promised Congress a hard line on inflation as June CPI came in at 3.5%—above the 2% target but cooling from May's 4.2%.
iShares SGOV ultra-short bond ETF is approaching $100B in assets and poised to become the first in its class to hit that milestone, reflecting heavy investor appetite for yield.
Vanguard and Envestnet partnered to minimize tax friction during model portfolio conversions, addressing a key pain point in RIA workflows.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 5 stories spanning incentive misalignment in sports, AI infrastructure financing, and legacy tech constraints—plus economic defenses of suburbs and a philosophical framework for context-dependent strategy.

bullets.text: NBA players deliberately take low-percentage end-of-quarter shots to pad individual stats at the expense of team value—a textbook principal-agent problem where performance metrics reward the wrong behavior.

Groq's Jonathan Ross engineered a $20B NVIDIA partnership out of near-bankruptcy using creative financing (Grok Bonds) and unconventional capital structures—a playbook for AI infrastructure startups facing cash crunches.
IBM's earnings miss roiled software stocks, but the deeper problem is that its mainframe cash cow—a legacy moat—is now a strategic straitjacket preventing aggressive AI competition.
Tyler Cowen argues badness is more universal while goodness is context-dependent—a useful frame for risk assessment and decision-making under uncertainty.

all.lede: Regional bank consolidation accelerates with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while payments sector faces potential upheaval from a $53B Stripe-Advent bid for PayPal.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaii lender's return to mainland U.S. following BNP Paribas exit.
Stripe and Advent Capital jointly bid $53B+ for PayPal with shares surging 19% pre-market; deal would consolidate two fintech giants and reshape payments competitive landscape.
Bank earnings season kicks off Tuesday with major lenders reporting amid elevated market expectations—watch for disappointment risk.
